Your friend says that the two ratios are equivalent. Is your friend correct? Explain your reasoning.

$$\times 6 \begin{pmatrix} 3 : 5 \\ 18 : 30 \end{pmatrix} \times 6$$

Because you can multiply each number in the first ratio by 6 to obtain the numbers in the second ratio, the ratios are equivalent.
answer_latex: Yes, your friend is correct. Because you can multiply each number in the first ratio by 6 to obtain the numbers in the second ratio, the ratios are equivalent.
